headline: Wall Street Saw Its Strongest Week 1 Earnings in Over 3 Years
summary: Bank of America strategists recapped the first week of earnings saying that with 50 S&P 500 companies reports in, 88% beat expectations.  UBS' CIO office also flagged stronger than historical averages for both beat rates and EPS growth, saying that the stock market has further to run thanks to what's expected to continue to be a strong earnings season.  "The wide gaps between individual stock performance and ongoing risks related to geopolitics and inflation, however, mean that investors should ensure diversified exposure," the strategists wrote.

raw.headline: Wall Street Saw Its Strongest Week 1 Earnings in Over 3 Years
raw.summary: Bank of America strategists recapped the first week of earnings saying that with 50 S&P 500 companies reports in, 88% beat expectations.  UBS' CIO office also flagged stronger than historical averages for both beat rates and EPS growth, saying that the stock market has further to run thanks to what's expected to continue to be a strong earnings season.  "The wide gaps between individual stock performance and ongoing risks related to geopolitics and inflation, however, mean that investors should ensure diversified exposure," the strategists wrote.

headline: Magnificent Seven Dominate S&P 500 Earnings, But That's Changing Dramatically
summary: The Magnificent Seven will again reign supreme over S&P 500 earnings, but their dominance is fading.  As second-quarter earnings reports start to flow in, analysts expect the Magnificent Seven companies to grow earnings by a combined 31.1%.  The other 493 S&P 500 companies are expected to increase earnings 22.8%, FactSet Senior Earnings Analyst John Butters wrote in a report.

summary: Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) released its company-compiled data of sell-side analysts’ forecasts for the automaker’s second-quarter 2026 earnings estimates ahead of this week’s earnings call. Analysts Predict $27 Billion in Revenue On Saturday, the Elon Musk-led automaker announced earnings forecasts from analysts at JP Morgan Chase & Co. (NYSE:JPM), Goldman Sachs Group Inc. (NYSE:GS) and several others. Read Also: This Week in Tesla: Elon Musk Faces Pay Package Questions, US EV Prices Fall and More
